title: astracantha thracica subsp. jankae
intro: Astracantha thracica subsp. jankae, also known as Jankaea thracica, is a plant species that belongs to the Astragalus genus of legumes. It is a perennial herb native to the Balkan Peninsula, traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other compounds, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits in treating various conditions, such as wounds, infections, and cardiovascular diseases. In herbal preparations, Astracantha thracica subsp. jankae is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is necessary to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
